description: §20.1/§20.8.2 (Client Router — navigate-soft-nav Wave-1b). A CLIENT `navigate("/reports")` inside a `<program>` shell compiles clean: it does NOT fire the spurious E-SCOPE-001 the builtin used to trip (it is a first-class navigation builtin, allowlisted in the logic-scope pass alongside render/log/print), no E-OUTLET-* placement error fires, and the shell renders the single addressable `[data-scrml-outlet]` swap region the soft-nav runtime targets.
rationale: §20.1: a client-side `navigate(path)` compiles to the soft-navigation pipeline (`_scrml_navigate_soft`), NOT a document reload; the call site must resolve as a builtin (no E-SCOPE-001), and the shell's `<outlet>` emits the `[data-scrml-outlet]` region the swap addresses (§20.8.2).
